name: Dr Patrick Jantz
school: School of Informatics, Computing, Cyber Systems
focus: Forest remote sensing • Ecosystem modeling • Biodiversity and conservation planning
bio: Patrick Jantz is an Assistant Research Professor at Northern Arizona University in the School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems, where he conducts research on forest ecology, geoinformatics, and remote sensing. His work focuses on understanding forest canopy structure, ecosystem dynamics, and biodiversity using advanced Earth observation technologies such as satellite data and LiDAR. Dr. Jantz integrates spatial analysis, ecological modeling, and machine learning to study how environmental change, land use, and climate dynamics impact ecosystems at regional and global scales. He is a member of the Global Earth Observation and Dynamics of Ecosystems (GEODE) Lab, where he contributes to research on carbon cycling, habitat connectivity, and conservation planning. His work also supports applied conservation efforts, including NASA-funded projects that use remote sensing data to model and protect wildlife habitats and guide sustainable development strategies.
